title: strychnos tetragona
intro: Strychnos tetragona is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, commonly known as the East African strychnine tree or strychnine bush. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in East Africa, due to their medicinal properties. The roots of Strychnos tetragona contain alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing and protection against infections. Strychnos tetragona is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its roots can be crushed and mixed with other herbs to create various herbal preparations for medicinal use.
